sentences_input.text: And although when our division was pulled out of there and I was reassigned to an entirely different unit because the war was now over, we knew that the division that would come in after us would also have to leave, so I befriended a captain from that division and made him promise that when the time came that the American troops would have to withdraw because the Russian troops would take over, to make it possible for Gerda and her friends to be evacuated to the American zone, which he promised and which in fact he-- he did.
So that-- I, in the meantime, had been reassigned to an intelligence center in-- in Bavaria, where I had some new duties.

But [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] it happened that one evening, I found out that Gerda and a friend of hers had in fact found me because I was in correspondence with this captain, had found me and had come to the town.
And I-- I found them a place to stay temporarily and then found them a place in Munich where they could stay more permanently.
And from then on, I would visit much more often, because now it was possible, although I-- I still ran into some things that now seem funny but weren't so funny at the time.
